data.title: European Commission releases 'Digital Omnibus' to streamline rules on AI, cybersecurity and data
data.description:
The European Commission released its Digital Omnibus package today, which it said is intended to streamline rules on artificial intelligence, cybersecurity and data across the member states.

"Closing the innovation gap and cutting red tape are key drivers to boost the EU's productivity," said Valdis Dombrovskis, European Commissioner for Economy and Productivity; Implementation and Simplification. "Our Digital Omnibus seeks to do both. Today's proposal represents an important first step in our digital simplification agenda, aiming to create a more favourable business environment for European companies."

The Digital Omnibus proposes several amendments to the AI Act, which are intended to simplify rules for companies pursuing AI innovations while still providing safeguards. It also aims to simplify cybersecurity reporting, reduce the number of cookie banners, and improve access to data.

The omnibus outlines a Data Union Strategy, which creates data labs providing AI applications and models expanded access while still protecting sensitive, non-personal data at home and abroad. It also introduces a European Business Wallet, which is expected to reduce administrative processes and costs.   

The package could reduce €5B in administrative costs by 2029, and the European European Business Wallets could unlock another €150B in savings for businesses each year, the Commission said. 

These updated laws could ease data access for AI training by big tech companies such as Meta Platforms (META), Google (GOOG)(GOOGL), OpenAI (OPENAI), SAP (SAP) and xAI (X.AI).

Tech companies and U.S. President Donald Trump have called on the EU to modify rules regarding some of its digital safeguards.

The digital omnibus will now be presented to the European Parliament and the Council for adoption. However, EU member nations are divided on these changes. A report last week by Politico said Estonia, France, Austria and Slovenia lawmakers oppose any changes to the General Data Protection Regulation. Meanwhile, lawmakers in Germany and Finland demonstrated support in order to help AI flourish in the EU.

data.title: Wix slumps despite Q3 beat and raising 2025 bookings outlook
data.description:
Shares of Wix.com (WIX) fell about 14% on Wednesday despite third quarter results beating estimates and the company raising its full year bookings outlook.

Wix also raised the lower end of its full year revenue outlook.

The Israel-based web development company's third quarter non-GAAP EPS rose 12% year-over-year to $1.68, while revenue jumped 14% year-over-year to $505.2M.

"Strong execution across both Creative Subscriptions and Business Solutions drove bookings and revenue growth to accelerate to 14% y/y, exceeding expectations. Importantly, new cohort bookings sustained the momentum we saw in 1H as a result of healthy demand and robust business fundamentals," said Wix's President and Co-Founder Nir Zohar.

The company's total bookings in the third quarter of 2025 grew 14% year-over-year to $514.5M.

Creative Subscriptions revenue climbed 12% year-over-year to $356.2M, while Business Solutions revenue surged 18% year-over-year to $149M. Partners revenue soared 24% year-over-year to $192.1M.

Stock Buyback program: Wix said it executed  $175M of share repurchases, buying back about 1.3 million ordinary shares in total at an average price of about  $136.64 per share.

Outlook

"As a result of Base44’s outperformance, coupled with new cohort strength in our core Wix business, we are increasing our bookings guidance for the full year. I’m confident the strategic investments we’re making today will enable Base44 to be a meaningful growth driver for Wix in 2026 and beyond with similar margins to our core business in the long-term," said Wix's CFO Lior Shemesh.

Wix acquired Base44 in June.

The company expects Base44 to achieve at least $50 million of Annual Recurring Revenue, or ARR, by year-end, an increase from the company's previous plan.

The company raised its full year bookings outlook to be between $2.06B and 2.078B, or 13-14% year-over-year growth, compared to a range of $2.040B to $2.075B, or 11-13% year-over-year  growth, previously.

Wix revised its full year revenue outlook to be between $1.99B and $2B, or 13-14% year-over-year growth, compared to a prior forecast range of $1.975B to $2B, or 12-14% year-over-year growth. The consensus revenue estimate is $1.99B.

The company expects non-GAAP gross margin to be 68% to 69% of revenue for the full year due to higher AI costs as a result of better than expected demand and increasing usage volume for Base44.

Wix anticipates free cash flow of about $600M in 2025, or 30% of revenue for the full year.

The company expects fourth quarter total revenue to be between $521M and $531M (midpoint at $526M), versus consensus of $526.04M.

Update: The story was updated with additional information and stock movement.

data.title: Archer Aviation lands a key eVTOL air mobility deal in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Archer Aviation (ACHR) is on watch after the company inked an agreement with The Helicopter Company to bring the company's Midnight air taxi to Saudi Arabia. Notably, The Helicopter Company is a subsidiary of Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund.

The partnership, which was announced at the Dubai Airshow, aims to create a sandbox framework for testing and integrating Archer's (ACHR) eVTOL aircraft within Saudi Arabia’s mobility ecosystem. The initiative will facilitate controlled real-world testing of the Midnight aircraft, focusing on operational feasibility, regulatory alignment, performance, passenger acceptance, and ecosystem preparedness. The collaboration is also designed to support Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030 goals by advancing urban air mobility and sustainable transportation under close regulatory oversight. 

As the partnership progresses, Red Sea Global will lead operational testing to assess the potential for large-scale eVTOL integration in its tourism and infrastructure projects. After the testing phase, the involved parties will evaluate results to consider broader rollouts of eVTOL services in Saudi Arabia. The new agreement was said to highlight Saudi Arabia’s ambitions to be a leader in next-generation, sustainable aviation, reinforcing commitments to innovation, diversification, and regenerative tourism. 

"A key part of Archer’s strategy is partnering with the leading operators in each country who share our goals and vision, and both THC and RSG are similarly committed to ensuring that Saudi Arabia is operationally prepared to launch eVTOL aircraft," stated Archer (ACHR) CEO Adam Goldstein on the deal.

Shares of Archer Aviation (ACH) edged 0.7% higher in early morning trading on Wednesday.

data.title: NanoVibronix issues letter to shareholders
data.description:
• NanoVibronix (NASDAQ:NAOV)  announced on Wednesday the issuance of a letter to shareholders, highlighting the company’s strategic vision, market opportunities, and a rebranding initiative.
  • To reflect our new strategic direction, the company is planning to rebrand its name from NanoVibronix to ENvue Medical, accompanied by a new NASDAQ ticker symbol.
  • The ENvue feeding-tube placement system, which was merged with the company in early 2025, was the strongest and most scalable growth platform. 
  • ENvue’s potential will be useful in pediatrics and preterm infants, X-ray navigation overlay, vascular access, and robotic tube placement. Together, these opportunities demonstrate that ENvue is more than a single product. It is a broad navigation technology platform with the potential to transform multiple critical-care procedures.
  • In the coming weeks, the company will introduce our Board of Directors following our Annual Meeting on December 4, 2025, announce key additions to our leadership team, and share early clinical progress that further validates the impact of ENvue.

data.title: Stocks inch up after the opening bell as investors look to Nvidia earnings
data.description:
Wall Street pushed slightly higher after the open on Wednesday, a day after posting its longest daily losing streak since August. All eyes are on AI darling Nvidia's (NVDA) earnings report amid continued weakness in the AI trade.

The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500), the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) were all marginally up.

After two days of risk-off selling on AI valuation and spending concerns, investors will likely continue to be cautious until Nvidia (NVDA) reports after the bell. 

Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid highlighted that Nvidia has now fallen more than 12% from its October 29 peak — its sharpest drop since the “Liberation Day” volatility earlier this year. He noted that even a major new AI deal with Microsoft and Anthropic failed to lift sentiment.

"Nvidia is the holy grail on which the market bases its mood – such a concentrated focus on one sector ((AI)) has practically never been seen before in this form. Valuations are hot, as are expectations. In short: caution, yes – panic, no," Marc-André Fongern, former Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs investment banker, said on X.

Meanwhile, the publication of delayed economic data after the government shutdown continued on Wednesday. The trade deficit in U.S. goods and services narrowed more than expected in August.

Looking at earnings-related moves, Target (TGT) was -0.3% after the retailer reported a decline in quarterly sales. Conversely, Lowe's (LOW) was +3.7% after the home improvement retailer raised its annual sales guidance.

data.title: Jeep unveils all-electric Recon SUV
data.description:
Despite a dramatic drop in electric vehicle sales in October and an end to government tax incentives, Stellantis (STLA) is forging ahead in the EV lineup with its latest entry, the all-electric 2026 Jeep Recon.

Built for off-roading with a 650 horsepower engine and 620 pounds of torque, the Recon is equipped with Jeep’s exclusive Selec-Terrain traction management system with five drive modes for all weather and road conditions.

The vehicle will begin production early next year at the Toluca, Mexico plant, with a sticker price starting at $65,000.

But with a range of just 250 miles fully charged, the Recon faces challenges with gas-powered off-road rivals that are not limited to the availability of charging stations, specifically from within the Jeep family.

Similar to the Jeep Wrangler, the Recon features removable doors, a spare tire affixed to the rear door, and open-air roof. But unlike the gas-powered and hybrid Wrangler – which sells for roughly $15,000 less -- the Recon is about 5 inches longer but with a six-inch shorter wheelbase and with less cargo room than the Wrangler.

Stellantis (STLA) shares are modestly higher into Wednesday’s open, potentially ending a five-day losing streak.

data.title: Blue Owl calls off merger of two private credit funds due to market conditions
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital Corp. (OBDC)  and Blue Owl Capital Corp. II terminated their previously announced proposed merger, citing current market conditions, but plans to reevaluate its options in the future, the companies said on Wednesday.

Blue Owl Capital Corp. (OBDC) stock gained 2.1% in premarket trading, while related entity, Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) stock rose 2.2%.

"Both funds remain strong, with excellent fundamentals, and we are confident in our ability to deliver attractive returns independently as we continue to work with the board to consider the best future opportunities for OBDC II," said Craig W. Packer, CEO of OBDC and OBDC II.

The firms came under pressure as investors sought to withdraw from the funds, the companies disclosed earlier this month.

Subject to board approval, OBDC II plans to reinstate the tender program in Q1 2026, it said.

OBDC's $200M share repurchase program, announced at the same time as the merger, remains in place.

OBDC is externally managed by Blue Owl Credit Advisors LLC, an investment adviser that's an indirect affiliate of Blue Owl Capital (OWL) and part of Blue Owl's credit platform.

data.title: European defense stocks fall on report of U.S. effort to end war in Ukraine
data.description:
European aerospace and defense shares slid sharply on Wednesday after reports suggested renewed U.S. efforts to broker an end to the Russia-Ukraine conflict, a development that also buoyed Ukrainian government bonds.

The STOXX Aerospace and Defense Index (EUAD) hit its weakest level since early September and was down about 2.6% in London, its steepest single-day decline in more than a month.

Major names including Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY), Renk, BAE Systems (OTCPK:BAESF) (OTCPK:BAESY), Leonardo (OTCPK:FINMF) (OTCPK:FINMY) and Saab (OTCPK:SAABF) (OTCPK:SAABY) posted losses of roughly 4% to 7%, underperforming a largely steady broader STOXX index.

A Politico report outlined a possible framework for peace talks, while a senior Ukrainian official told Reuters that Kyiv had received initial indications of U.S. ideas being discussed with Russia.

data.title: Vaso announces divestiture of VasoHealthcare for $0.8M
data.description:
• Vaso (OTCQX:VASO) announced on Wednesday that it had reached an agreement with Nano-X Imaging to sell one of its subsidiaries, VasoHealthcare IT.
  • The transaction will be considered up to $0.8M, with $0.2M payable at closing and up to $0.6M as an earnout based on the post-closing performance of the business.
  • The divestiture of VasoHealthcare IT will allow the company to focus on its core operations and competencies.
  • The company expects to use the proceeds from the sale to invest in its other business lines and for strategic initiatives.
